root_cause.text: The IPv6 sysctl handler for setting the MTU (`mtu6`) uses `proc_dointvec` instead of a bounds-checking handler, allowing userspace to set invalid MTU values that are smaller than the IPv6 minimum or larger than the interface's underlying MTU.
root_cause.evidence: The patch replaces `proc_dointvec` with a custom `addrconf_sysctl_mtu` handler that utilizes `proc_dointvec_minmax` to constrain the new MTU between `IPV6_MIN_MTU` and `idev->dev->mtu`.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The IPv6 sysctl handler for setting the MTU (`mtu6`) uses `proc_dointvec` instead of a bounds-checking handler, allowing userspace to set invalid MTU values that are smaller than the IPv6 minimum or larger than the interface's underlying MTU.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch replaces `proc_dointvec` with a custom `addrconf_sysctl_mtu` handler that utilizes `proc_dointvec_minmax` to constrain the new MTU between `IPV6_MIN_MTU` and `idev->dev->mtu`.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add a sysctl handler to enforce minimum and maximum bounds on IPv6 MTU settings.
intent_record.what: Adds a new sysctl handler function 'addrconf_sysctl_mtu' that uses 'proc_dointvec_minmax' to process MTU changes.
intent_record.how: It enforces boundary checks on the MTU value, ensuring it is not set below IPV6_MIN_MTU or above the underlying device's MTU.

intent_record.intent_summary: Replace the generic sysctl integer handler with a specialized IPv6 MTU handler to ensure proper validation.
intent_record.what: Changed the proc_handler for the mtu6 sysctl entry from the generic proc_dointvec to a specific addrconf_sysctl_mtu function.
intent_record.how: Routes MTU sysctl modifications through a dedicated handler, allowing for custom validation and necessary side-effects when the IPv6 MTU is changed.

grouping_result.intent: Replace the generic sysctl integer handler with a specialized IPv6 MTU handler to ensure proper validation.

consistency_review.core_intent: Implement and use a specialized sysctl handler to validate IPv6 MTU bounds.

relevance_review.reason: The group directly addresses the vulnerability by adding and applying a specialized sysctl handler to enforce bounds checking on IPv6 MTU settings, preventing invalid values.

candidates.rationale: The sysctl handler for mtu6 is set to proc_dointvec, which does not perform bounds checking, allowing invalid MTU values to be set.

vuln_statements.explanation: This statement assigns the generic proc_dointvec handler to the mtu6 sysctl, which fails to enforce bounds checking for the MTU values, allowing invalid settings.

Fix some of the checkpatch complaints.

Signed-off-by: Stephen Hemminger <shemminger@vyatta.

Author: Stephen Hemminger <shemminger@vyatta.com>
Date:   Sat Mar 20 16:09:01 2010 -0700

    addrconf: checkpatch fixes
    
    Fix some of the checkpatch complaints.
    
    Signed-off-by: Stephen Hemminger <shemminger@vyatta.com>
    Signed-off-by: David S. Miller <davem@davemloft.net>

diff --git a/net/ipv6/addrconf.c b/net/ipv6/addrconf.c
index bcb55b78746f..279580eab309 100644
--- a/net/ipv6/addrconf.c
+++ b/net/ipv6/addrconf.c
@@ -81,7 +81
bic_trace_step.reason: The sysctl entry for 'mtu' (setting ipv6_devconf.mtu6) still uses 'proc_dointvec' as its proc_handler, lacking the necessary bounds checking for valid MTU values.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg:
net: '&' redux

I want to compile out proc_* and sysctl_* handlers totally and
stub them to NULL depending on config opt
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 6d9f239a1edb31d6133230f478fd1dc2da338ec5
Author: Alexey Dobriyan <adobriyan@gmail.com>
Date:   Mon Nov 3 18:21:05 2008 -0800

    net: '&' redux
    
    I want to compile out proc_* and sysctl_* handlers totally and
    stub them to NULL depending on config options, however usage of &
    will prevent this, since taking adress of NULL pointer will break
    compilation.
    
    So, drop & in front of every ->proc_handler and every ->strategy
    handler, it was never needed in fact.
bic_trace_step.reason: The sysctl table entry for 'mtu' (.data = &ipv6_devconf.mtu6) assigns 'proc_dointvec' to '.proc_handler' instead of a bounds-checking handler, matching the vulnerability root cause.
